This looks like a spiral pattern.
The initial state is a straight vertical line.
The goal is a spiral.
The domain allows rotating the string at a node.
The action `rotate` takes a node `?n`, a rotation `?r`, a `?fromdir`, and a `?todir`.
It changes the heading of `?n` from `?fromdir` to `?todir`.
It sets up the first pass cursor `node-first-pass-next ?n ?r ?n`.
Then `rotate-first-pass` propagates the changes along the connected nodes.
It updates headings and coordinates.
It deletes old `at` facts and sets `free` facts.
It updates the cursor to the next node.
`rotate-first-pass-end` handles the end of the string in the first pass.
`rotate-second-pass` sets the coordinates back based on the new headings.
`rotate-second-pass-end` finishes the rotation.
